title: Baked Portuguese Custard Tarts
description: A traditional Portuguese dessert, these egg-based custard tarts are baked to perfection and topped with a caramelized sugar crust. This recipe is vegan-friendly and perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of about 3mm (1/8 inch).
Cut out circles of pastry dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place the pastry circles into a muffin tin, pressing them into the corners.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the sugar, flour, and vanilla extract.
Add the almond milk to the bowl and whisk until smooth.
Pour the mixture into the pastry-lined muffin tin.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes.
Use a skewer to create a hole in the center of each tart, then use a spoon to carefully remove the center of each tart.
Return the tarts to the oven and bake for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the centers are firm and the edges are golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool completely before serving.
